Title: AI Visibility for Umbraco
Description: Make your Umbraco site visible to AI search engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, Bing Copilot) and give editors visibility into AI traffic. Drop-in for Umbraco v17+ with zero configuration on a typical site. Every published page becomes reachable as Markdown via Accept: text/markdown content negotiation or by appending .md to the URL. /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt manifests are auto-generated per the llmstxt.org spec — hot-path-protected with If-None-Match / 304 / single-flight caching. Two Backoffice dashboards appear under Settings: AI Visibility (site name, summary, per-doctype + per-page exclusion) and AI Traffic (read-only dashboard logging every AI / human / crawler hit by classification, with date filtering). A Health Check warns when /robots.txt blocks AI crawlers you might not want to block — the package never modifies robots.txt for you, it audits and surfaces. The architectural principle: the Umbraco template is the canonical visual form of content. The package renders pages through Umbraco's normal Razor pipeline and converts the resulting HTML to Markdown — whatever your templates produce (Block List, Block Grid, view components) is what the AI gets, in clean Markdown form. Six DI extension points let you override extraction, request logging, robots auditing, settings resolution, and UA classification without forking. Apache 2.0 licensed.

AuthorDetails.Name: Adam Shallcross
AuthorDetails.Description: CEO & Founder at Cogworks. Umbraco advocate, interested in how humans and AI can work together in harmony.
